digest.component_or_area: Gerrit serial auto-lander (project infra + land CLI)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on FFO main force manual rebase-and-retry, causing redundant CI runs and wasted agent attention.
A serial auto-lander service rebases the front Autosubmit change/chain to tip, awaits a fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact CI-tested SHA.

A single land command returns one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed), where needs_rebase is persisted by the bot and the others are derived live from Gerrit primitives.
The two-vote gate and FFO backstop are never weakened: nothing lands whose to-be-merged tree wasn't CI-tested, and the whole stack is handed back on failure rather than partial-landed.
No Gerrit-specific landing logic is added to src/rebar core; the lander lives in project infra and the provider-seam generalization is deferred.
This serial auto-lander architecture was strategically retired and superseded by a server-side Rebase-If-Necessary approach (epic d4de, ADR-0047).
